paper_evidence.summary: Two post-transplant refractory MM patients with BRAF V600E showed rapid biochemical/clinical (Pt1) and biochemical+radiographic (Pt2) responses to vemurafenib 960 mg BID; Pt1 later developed CNS progression ~7 weeks; Pt2 ongoing response at 4 months with dermatitis and SCC during therapy.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Pt1: pain improved within 4 days; IgG κ 2.5→1.3 g/dL; κ FLC 41→7 mg/dL within 1 month; CNS involvement ~7 weeks. Pt2: κ FLC ↓90% within 1 month; normalized at 2 months; PET near-resolution; ongoing at 4 months; grade 1 dermatitis; SCC treated.

fields.evidence_description: Matches Pt1: rapid pain improvement; IgG κ and κ FLC reductions within 1 month; response ~7 weeks then CNS progression.
fields.verbatim_quote: Exact sentence block from Pt1 case presentation describing pain improvement and biomarker drops.

ai_items.notes: Core clinical claim is faithful. Major issues: evidence_level not CIViC-consistent (case study should be C), and NCT ID does not match paper text; FAERS safety list is not sourced to the paper.

fields.evidence_description: Matches Pt2: 90% κ FLC decrease at 1 month, normalization at 2 months, PET near-resolution, ongoing response at 4 months.
fields.verbatim_quote: Exact sentence block from Pt2 case presentation describing 90% drop, normalization, PET response, 4-month ongoing response.

ai_items.notes: Core efficacy claim is faithful. Misses paper-specific adverse events (dermatitis, squamous cell carcinoma). NCT ID mismatch and CIViC evidence_level mismatch remain.

gt_items.notes: Covers Pt1 but is underspecified: does not include pain/biomarker response magnitude, and frames outcome mainly as relapse/death at ~7 weeks.
Covers Pt2 and correctly notes near-resolution at 4 months, but misses the explicit 90%/normalization timeline and AEs.

paper_evidence.summary: Paper states BRAF V600E-mutated MM may have aggressive clinical course with prominent extramedullary disease and short duration response to standard therapies; cites Andrulis et al (7/379; extramedullary 4/7) and notes both case patients had aggressive/extramedullary features.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Andrulis et al summary: 7/379 BRAF V600E; 4/7 extramedullary (as reported in this paper’s discussion).

fields.evidence_description: Aggressive phenotype + extramedullary disease; cites Andrulis 7/379 and notes both case patients aggressive.
fields.verbatim_quote: Matches discussion sentence(s) about aggressive phenotype and Andrulis cohort summary.

ai_items.notes: Core prognostic statement is supported by the paper, but (1) cohort_size=9 mixes external Andrulis cohort with in-paper cases in a way that is not clean for a single-source evidence item, and (2) NCT field does not belong here and also mismatches paper.

missing_from_ai: Paper-specific adverse events during vemurafenib in Patient 2 (grade 1 dermatitis; squamous cell carcinoma requiring treatment) are not captured as structured safety evidence.
ai_hallucinated_items: FAERS/OpenFDA 'top_adverse_events' list is not in the paper; it is external enrichment and should not be treated as paper-supported evidence.
Clinical trial ID is mismatched (paper text shows NCT01524978, AI uses NCT01524972).
ai_improvements_over_gt: Adds a supported prognostic/aggressive-phenotype item not present in ground truth.
Adds quantitative response metrics and timelines (pain improvement; IgG/κ FLC changes; PET near-resolution; CNS progression timing).
Adds normalization/enrichment fields (HGVS protein, Entrez/EFO/NCIt/RxCUI) that are absent from ground truth.

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction captures the core clinical claims of the paper very well for the two predictive case items and meaningfully extends beyond ground truth by adding a supported prognostic statement. However, it has key normalization/metadata errors (notably the clinical trial NCT mismatch and CIViC evidence-level mismatch) and fails to encode paper-specific adverse events while instead attaching an external FAERS safety list not supported by the paper.
strengths: High fidelity to the paper’s efficacy narratives and quantitative response details for both patients.
Correct direction/significance for predictive response and prognostic poor-outcome framing.
Adds a real (paper-supported) evidence item missing from CIViC baseline.
weaknesses: Incorrect clinical trial identifier vs paper text.
Evidence_level not aligned with CIViC grading for case reports (should be C).
Safety handling is inverted: misses in-paper AEs but adds external FAERS adverse-event frequencies not paper-derived.
Prognostic cohort_size field is not cleanly defined for a single-source evidence item (mixes cohorts across publications).
